text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Elderglen, there lived a peculiar woman named Harriet Humbug. She didn’t belong. She wore bowlers even in summer, her coat perpetually a shade of mustard yellow. Her house was a whimsical pile of mismatched furniture, each piece telling a quirky story. Harriet loved gossip and puzzles, frequently engaging in debates about the most absurd subjects.

One breezy afternoon, while flit-crawling from one gossip fest to another, Harriet heard whispers about the annual Harvest Banquet. It was said that the local scholar, Gilbert Flitterwings, held these events to discuss ancient mysteries. Curiosity piqued, Harriet plotted a visit.

Upon arrival, Harriet approached the hall adorned with cobwebs and antique trophies. The grand piano stood center stage, its keys untouched for eons. On one side, a table piled high with pumpkin-carved masks. On the other, Gilbert, shrouded in
